name: similarity-search-patterns
description: Implement efficient similarity search with vector databases. Use when building semantic search, implementing nearest neighbor queries, or optimizing retrieval performance.
capabilities: Provide vector store implementations for Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate
Explain distance metrics (cosine, euclidean, dot product, manhattan) and index types (Flat, HNSW, IVF+PQ)
Generate hybrid search queries combining vector and keyword search
Implement reranking with cross-encoder models for improved relevance
Guide index parameter tuning for recall and latency tradeoffs
Create filterable vector search with metadata conditions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 31 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged Markdown documentation syntax as executable code. Backticks are code fence markers, URLs are documentation links, and API key parameters represent secure credential handling. The skill contains Python code examples for vector database implementations with no security concerns.
valueStatement: Vector similarity search is essential for semantic search and RAG systems but requires careful index selection and tuning. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for Pinecone, Qdrant, pgvector, and Weaviate with hybrid search and reranking.
userTitle: Build production similarity search with vector databases
limitations: Does not include embedding generation - requires separate embedding model or API
Code examples are Python-focused - other languages need adaptation
Does not cover vector quantization training or custom index building
Cloud service costs and quotas are not addressed

useCases.targetUser: AI application developers building chatbots or search systems
useCases.title: RAG Application Builder
useCases.description: Implement retrieval-augmented generation with efficient vector search over document embeddings. Supports hybrid search combining semantic and keyword matching for accurate context retrieval.

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers creating product or content recommendations
useCases.title: Recommendation Engine Developer
useCases.description: Build nearest-neighbor recommendation systems using vector embeddings. Leverage HNSW indexing for sub-second queries over millions of items with configurable recall thresholds.

useCases.targetUser: Data platform teams scaling search infrastructure
useCases.title: Search Platform Engineer
useCases.description: Deploy production similarity search infrastructure with PostgreSQL pgvector or managed services. Implement pre-filtering and reranking pipelines for enterprise search quality.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Vector Store Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to store and search document embeddings. Help me set up a Pinecone vector index with cosine similarity for 1536-dimensional embeddings. Include upsert and search functions with metadata filtering.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new RAG project with OpenAI embeddings

promptTemplates.title: Hybrid Search Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hybrid search function that combines vector similarity with keyword matching using pgvector. Weight vector search at 0.7 and full-text search at 0.3. Include the SQL schema with HNSW index.
promptTemplates.scenario: Improving search relevance by combining semantic and lexical matching

promptTemplates.title: Reranking Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: I have 50 vector search results but need better ranking. Show me how to rerank with a cross-encoder model. Include the sentence-transformers code and explain the tradeoff between latency and accuracy.
promptTemplates.scenario: Production RAG system requiring high-precision retrieval

promptTemplates.title: Index Selection and Tuning
promptTemplates.prompt: I have 10 million vectors and need P99 latency under 100ms with 95% recall. Recommend an index type and parameters for Qdrant. Explain ef_construct, nprobe, and quantization settings for my use case.
promptTemplates.scenario: Scaling vector search from prototype to production workload

bestPractices: Start with flat index for small datasets (under 100K vectors) and migrate to HNSW as data grows
Always measure recall at your target latency before deploying approximate indexes
Use metadata pre-filtering to reduce search space before vector comparison
antiPatterns: Using approximate nearest neighbor indexes without measuring recall degradation
Storing raw vectors without quantization when memory is constrained
Querying without pre-filters causing full index scans on large collections

faq.question: Which vector database should I choose for my project?
faq.answer: For quick prototypes, use Pinecone (managed, no infra). For PostgreSQL shops, pgvector adds minimal complexity. For high-performance needs, Qdrant or Weaviate offer advanced features. Consider data size, query latency requirements, and existing infrastructure.

faq.question: What is the difference between cosine similarity and dot product?
faq.answer: Cosine similarity measures angle only (ignores magnitude), making it ideal for normalized embeddings. Dot product includes magnitude effects. For most embedding models (OpenAI, Sentence Transformers), cosine similarity is the standard choice.

faq.question: How do I improve search recall without sacrificing latency?
faq.answer: Increase index parameters (ef_search for HNSW, nprobe for IVF) gradually while measuring P99 latency. Use reranking to recover precision after high-recall retrieval. Consider two-stage retrieval: fast approximate search followed by exact reranking on top-K results.

faq.question: Can I update vectors after insertion?
faq.answer: Yes, all supported vector databases support upsert operations. Re-inserting a vector with the same ID updates it in place. Note that frequent updates may require index rebuilding for optimal performance.

faq.question: What embedding dimension should I use?
faq.answer: Use the native dimension of your embedding model (e.g., 1536 for text-embedding-ada-002, 384 for all-MiniLM-L6-v2). Higher dimensions capture more nuance but increase storage and compute. Match your index metric to the embedding model's training objective.

faq.question: How do I handle multilingual search?
faq.answer: Use multilingual embedding models like multilingual-e5 or LaBSE that map different languages to the same vector space. Query and documents in different languages will still retrieve relevant results if the embedding model supports them.
